Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Aug-17-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: PHILIP J. SMITH, ESQ. FOR INTERVENOR SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT(JOINDER)/MOVING PARTY. DAVID E. MACKENROTH, ESQ. FOR RICHARD AND VALERIE GIRLING, PLAINTIFFS/MOVING PARTIES. SCOTT D. WIENER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. GEOFFREY GORDON-CREED, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, JENIECE FEE
RULING:
MATTER ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATIONS. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 17 and 18, limited to the period 5 years prior to the accident. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 21. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 30, 31, 32, 33, and 34. The motion is moot as to Request for Production, Set One, Nos. 3 as Defendant has produced the documents. The motion is granted as to Request For Production No. 4, limited to 5 years prior to accident. The motion is denied as to Request For Production No. 5. As to the privilege log, the motion is moot as to documents 0051 as the objections have been withdrawn. The motion is denied as to documents 0070-71 and 0072-75. The motion is withdrawn as to document 0082. The motion is denied as to the personnel file documents 0123-0498, except for those items allowed through orders on specific interrogatories and document requests. The motion is denied as to documents 0499-0503, 0504, and 0506. The motion is denied as to Request For Production No. 7, except for those items allowed in response to specific interrogatories and document requests. The motion is granted as to Request For Production No. 9. Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. MODIFICATIONS: PRODUCTION OF PERSONNEL RECORDS IS SUBJECT TO CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER AND PRODUCTION SHALL BE WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ORDER. (612/EAH)
